extra.message: User-supplied input from request.POST['first_name'] is base64-decoded and passed directly to exec(). This allows arbitrary Python code execution on the server. An attacker can encode any Python code in base64 and have it executed with the privileges of the web server process.

cwe: CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code ('Code Injection')

extra.message: User-supplied input from request.GET['p'] is joined directly with a base path using os.path.join() and passed to open() without any sanitization. An attacker can use path traversal sequences (e.g., '../../etc/passwd') to read arbitrary files on the server filesystem that the web process has read access to.

cwe: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')

extra.message: User-supplied URL from request.GET['url'] is passed directly to redirect() with no validation or whitelisting of allowed destinations. An attacker can craft a link that redirects victims to an arbitrary external site, enabling phishing attacks.

cwe: CWE-601: URL Redirection to Untrusted Site ('Open Redirect')

extra.message: User-supplied input from request.GET['fwd'] is used as a key into globals() to look up and call a function. This allows an attacker to invoke any Python function in the global scope, including the admin() function at line 191 that renders an admin page without any authentication check. An attacker can access privileged views by passing their function name as the 'fwd' parameter.

User-supplied query parameter 'qs' (from request.GET) is rendered into a JavaScript block using the |safe filter, which disables Django's auto-escaping. An attacker can break out of the JavaScript string and inject arbitrary JavaScript, enabling stored or reflected XSS.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: User-supplied query parameter 'qs' is rendered into an HTML input field value attribute using the |safe filter. This disables Django's HTML escaping and allows an attacker to inject arbitrary HTML/JavaScript by breaking out of the attribute value, enabling reflected XSS.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: URL path variable 'path' captured by the regex route is rendered into a JavaScript block using the |safe filter. An attacker can craft a URL path containing JavaScript that breaks out of the string literal and executes arbitrary JavaScript in the victim's browser.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: User-supplied query parameter 'qs' is rendered into a JavaScript variable assignment using the |safe filter in the csrf_image view. This allows reflected XSS and is also used to demonstrate CSRF-via-XSS, since the csrf_image endpoint is decorated with @csrf_exempt.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: The csrf_image view is decorated with @csrf_exempt, completely disabling Django's CSRF protection for this endpoint. Combined with the XSS vulnerability in the same template, this enables cross-site request forgery attacks where a third-party page can cause an authenticated user's browser to perform unintended state-changing requests.

The Django CSRF middleware 'django.middleware.csrf.CsrfViewMiddleware' is commented out in MIDDLEWARE_CLASSES. This disables CSRF protection globally for the entire application, making every state-changing POST endpoint vulnerable to cross-site request forgery attacks.

The missing_access_control view renders an admin page when the query parameter 'action' equals 'admin'. There is no authentication check whatsoever — any unauthenticated user can access the admin page by appending '?action=admin' to the URL.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: The dor_user_profile view accepts a userid from the URL path and uses it to look up and modify user data without any authorization check. Any user can view or modify any other user's profile (including admin accounts) by simply changing the numeric ID in the URL, e.g., /direct-object-references/users/2.

cwe: CWE-639: Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key

extra.message: DEBUG = True is set in settings.py. In debug mode Django exposes full stack traces including local variable values, settings, and installed apps to any visitor who triggers an error. This can leak sensitive configuration, secret keys, and internal paths.

cwe: CWE-209: Generation of Error Message Containing Sensitive Information

extra.message: The Django SECRET_KEY is hardcoded as a plaintext string in settings.py. If the source code is exposed (e.g., via the path traversal vulnerability in this same app, or a public repository), an attacker can use this key to forge session cookies and CSRF tokens, potentially leading to full authentication bypass.

The X-Frame-Options clickjacking protection middleware 'django.middleware.clickjacking.XFrameOptionsMiddleware' is commented out. Without this middleware no X-Frame-Options header is sent, allowing the application to be embedded in iframes on attacker-controlled pages to facilitate clickjacking attacks.

cwe: CWE-1021: Improper Restriction of Rendered UI Layers or Frames

extra.message: The xss_form view sets a cookie named 'monster' using response.set_cookie() without specifying httponly=True or secure=True. This cookie is accessible via JavaScript (document.cookie), which is exactly what the XSS exercise exploits. Sensitive cookies should always set the HttpOnly flag to prevent JavaScript access.

The Django Debug Toolbar show_toolbar callback allows the toolbar to be enabled by any user who supplies a 'debug' parameter in GET, POST, or a cookie. This exposes full SQL queries, request/response headers, template context, and settings to any external attacker who can set this parameter.

cwe: CWE-200: Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorized Actor
